messages.content:
What is the core function of an enterprise platform?


It hosts all businesses and other platforms.


It connects the supplier with the consumer.


It manages the primary functions of a business.


It helps employees interact with their supervisors.


I don't know this yet.

The core function of an enterprise platform is to manage the primary functions of a business. It typically includes functionalities like data storage, workflow management, collaboration tools, customer relationship management, and other components necessary for the efficient operation of a company.

Which describes a platform?


an organized collection of data stored electronically on a local computer system or cloud


a framework that provides foundational working system for software development


a ready-to-use software package that performs a specific business function


a piece of technology that connects people and other technologies together  


I don't know this yet.

A platform is generally described as a framework that provides a foundational working system for software development. It is a base on which developers can build and deploy applications, leveraging pre-existing tools, libraries, and services. A platform typically provides the infrastructure, development tools, and frameworks needed to facilitate the creation, deployment, and maintenance of software applications.

Which two companies are among the "Big Six" enterprise platform partners used by Accenture's Intelligent Platform Services (IPS) business?


IBM and Cisco


Facebook and Twitter


Google and Instagram


Oracle and Workday


I don't know this yet.
Oracle and Workday are two companies among the "Big Six" enterprise platform partners used by Accenture's Intelligent Platform Services (IPS) business.

Which describes the relationship between enterprise platforms and the cloud?


Enterprise platforms are primarily built around and hosted on the cloud.


Data on the cloud can be analyzed and monitored without the need for a platform.


All enterprise platforms are cloud-based.


Enterprise platforms are an alternative to hosting solutions on the cloud.


I don't know this yet.

The relationship between enterprise platforms and the cloud varies. While some enterprise platforms are primarily built around and hosted on the cloud, not all enterprise platforms are cloud-based. The cloud can be used as a hosting solution for enterprise platforms, but there are also on-premises or hybrid platform options available. The use of cloud infrastructure enables scalability, flexibility, and accessibility for enterprise platforms, but it is not the only way these platforms can be hosted or utilized.

A company is considering several Software as a Service (SaaS) platform providers to manage their talent lifecycle of hiring, onboarding, ongoing career development, and payroll.

Where should the company primarily focus when evaluating different SaaS providers?


on the provider’s underlying infrastructure


on the geographic location of the data centers


on the capacity of the provider’s data storage


on the features of the software itself


I don't know this yet.

When evaluating different Software as a Service (SaaS) platform providers for managing the talent lifecycle, the company should primarily focus on the features of the software itself. This means considering the functionality, usability, customizability, integration capabilities, reporting and analytics, security features, and overall suitability of the software for the company's specific talent management needs. While underlying infrastructure, geographic location of data centers, and data storage capacity are important factors to consider, the features and capabilities of the software should be the main focus as they directly impact the company's ability to effectively manage their talent lifecycle.

A start-up is expanding overseas and spends an excessive amount of time on recruiting and hiring activities, hindering its ability to focus on the core aspects of its business. How can a Human Capital Management (HCM) platform provider benefit this company?


by outsourcing the company’s entire Human Resources function to industry experts


by providing a customizable Human Resources system that can be tailored to the company’s needs


by offering a standard Human Resources application hosted on the company’s servers


by building a fully unique Human Resources system from the ground up


I don't know this yet.

A Human Capital Management (HCM) platform provider can benefit the start-up expanding overseas by providing a customizable Human Resources system that can be tailored to the company's needs. This platform can streamline and automate various HR processes, including recruiting and hiring activities. By using an HCM platform, the start-up can centralize and digitize HR-related tasks, saving time and effort. The platform may include features such as applicant tracking, interview scheduling, onboarding workflows, and employee data management. This allows the company to focus on its core business aspects while efficiently managing its HR functions through the platform's customizable features.

A large public utilities company approaches Accenture about an enterprise platform. The company's business functions are currently split across multiple older legacy systems, and the company wishes to improve the efficiency of its overall operations.

How can an Accenture Intelligent Platform Services (IPS) help the client?


by coordinating direct negotiations between the client and external platform providers


by assigning separate teams of software engineers to re-program the client's legacy systems


by migrating and integrating the client's existing data into an enterprise platform


by transferring the client's legacy systems to an Accenture cloud-based server


I don't know this yet.

An Accenture Intelligent Platform Services (IPS) can help the client, a large public utilities company, by migrating and integrating their existing data into an enterprise platform. The primary goal is to improve the efficiency of their overall operations by consolidating their business functions, which are currently spread across multiple older legacy systems. Accenture will assist in transferring and integrating the client's data from these legacy systems into a modern enterprise platform. This would enable the company to have a unified and streamlined system for better management and operational efficiency.

A Human Resources Manager is looking for a solution that will streamline the tasks involved in hiring, payroll, and employee requests for time off.

Which SaaS platform would benefit the Manager in this situation?


Workday


ServiceNow


Zendesk


Salesforce


I don't know this yet.

Out of the provided options, Workday would likely be the most appropriate SaaS platform to benefit the Human Resources Manager in streamlining tasks related to hiring, payroll, and employee requests for time off. Workday is a popular cloud-based HCM (Human Capital Management) platform that offers various modules for HR management, including recruitment, onboarding, time tracking, and payroll. It is designed to provide comprehensive HR solutions, making it suitable for managing the tasks mentioned by the HR Manager. However, it's important to note that other platforms like ServiceNow, Zendesk, or Salesforce may also offer integrations or modules specifically for HR management, so further evaluation of features and suitability would be recommended.

An Accenture client is currently using Salesforce as its Customer Relationship Management (CRM) solution. The client would like to implement an AI application to help predict future customer behavior.

Which approach takes advantage of Salesforce's capabilities?


Contracting with the AI application's developer to build a standalone solution.


Asking Salesforce to develop a custom AI application as part of its standard offering.


Running the AI application on a separate server and uploading the results to Salesforce daily.


Integrating the AI application directly into the Salesforce framework.


I don't know this yet.

To take advantage of Salesforce's capabilities and implement an AI application to predict future customer behavior, the most suitable approach would be to integrate the AI application directly into the Salesforce framework. Salesforce provides various options for integrating third-party applications and technologies, including AI capabilities. By integrating the AI application directly into Salesforce, it can leverage the existing data and functionalities within the CRM system, allowing for seamless data exchange and enhancing the predictive capabilities within the Salesforce environment. This integration ensures that the AI application can make use of Salesforce's data and tools effectively, providing valuable insights into customer behavior.

An online retailer is looking to implement an enterprise platform. Which component of the enterprise platform will help the company capture, curate, and consume customer information to improve their services?


Experience


Values and Principles


Core Process


Data and Insights


I don't know this yet.

The component of the enterprise platform that will help the online retailer capture, curate, and consume customer information to improve their services is "Data and Insights." This component focuses on managing and analyzing customer data, enabling the retailer to gain valuable insights into customer behavior, preferences, and trends. By capturing and curating customer information through data collection and analysis, the retailer can make data-driven decisions to enhance their services, personalize customer experiences, and optimize their overall business strategies. Data and insights play a crucial role in improving customer satisfaction, targeting marketing efforts, and making informed business decisions for the online retailer.

Which describes the "Network Effect?"


A platform becomes more useful as it is linked to more applications.


A platform becomes more useful as its total computing power increases.


A platform can lose effectiveness if too many users are logged in simultaneously.


A platform becomes more useful as more people join and use it.


I don't know this yet.

The "Network Effect" refers to the phenomenon where a platform becomes more useful as more people join and use it. As the number of users increases, the platform gains value because it offers more opportunities for interaction, collaboration, and exchange of goods or services. This increased user base creates a positive feedback loop where each additional user enhances the overall utility and benefits of the platform for all participants. The network effect is commonly observed in social media platforms, communication platforms, marketplaces, and other types of platforms where user participation and engagement play a significant role in the platform's value and success.

A company is considering several Customer Relationship Management (CRM) providers and wants to understand what customizations each platform can offer. What is a good indicator of a CRM software’s customization capability?


the market share held by the CRM platform provider


the amount of user traffic the CRM platform can handle


the quality of the user interface and user experience


the robustness of the Application Programming Interface (API)


I don't know this yet.

A good indicator of a CRM software's customization capability is the robustness of its Application Programming Interface (API). The API allows third-party developers to extend and customize the functionality of the CRM platform. A robust API gives more flexibility to integrate and customize the CRM software with other applications and systems, enabling the company to tailor the CRM solution to its specific needs. It allows for the development of custom features, workflows, and integrations that can enhance the CRM's capabilities and adapt it to the company's unique requirements. Therefore, the quality and extensibility of the API are important considerations when assessing a CRM platform's customization capabilities.

Which process of the enterprise platform interacts with vendors and manages the entire supply chain for a retail client?


Front Office


Core Processing


Data


Experience


I don't know this yet.

The process of the enterprise platform that interacts with vendors and manages the entire supply chain for a retail client is known as "Core Processing." This process involves managing various operations related to the supply chain, including procurement, inventory management, order fulfillment, logistics, and vendor management. Core Processing within an enterprise platform ensures seamless coordination and integration of activities across the supply chain, optimizing efficiency and reducing costs for the retail client. It aims to streamline the flow of goods and information between vendors, suppliers, and the retail company, ultimately delivering a smooth and effective supply chain management system.
